name: shrine-roadside
summary: A small devotional structure along a road or trail. Often open to the elements. Offerings from many passing travelers layered over time.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: very small — the shrine is the room

primary_palette.note: weathered stone altar with carved symbol
patron deity effigy on a pedestal

story_prompts: fresh offering from a traveler who passed an hour ago
neglected for a season — offerings decayed, candle-stubs not replaced
shrine to a forgotten or forbidden god — signs of furtive recent worship
the site of a local miracle — extra offerings, more than a roadside would warrant

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: natural/outdoor lit (0.5+) or unlit under cover

anti_patterns: keep it tiny — this is a waypoint, not a chapel
offerings layered across eras (some fresh, some rotted) — never all one vintage
no seating — pilgrims kneel or stand briefly
